summary: All 4 functions passed with 0 compile-fix cycles total. 11 proof blocks generated, 116 LOC. Exec block generation is reliable for BOUNDS_PROPAGATION, POSTCONDITION_CHAINING, and SCALAR_BIT_BRIDGE patterns.

block_identification.notes: All agents correctly identified block positions from function structure alone

callee_lookup.E2_used_sibling_function: mul_by_pow_2 (same as_projective→double→as_extended chain) served as template
callee_lookup.E4_pattern_source: Intact blocks 1-2 and 9+ in same function

E1.approach_vs_gt: Used extensional equality (=~=) instead of array literal. Used lemma_add_bounds_propagate instead of bit_vector assertions. Different but cleaner.

E2.approach_vs_gt: Block 2 empty (GT had 4 lines). Agent recognized postconditions chain directly. Found sibling function mul_by_pow_2 as template. Used by-blocks instead of linear assertions.

E3.approach_vs_gt: Same logical chain but more concise (38 vs 47 LOC). Better commented with step-by-step annotations. Eliminated redundant intermediate assertions.

E4.approach_vs_gt: Used by-blocks for cleaner structure. Block 8 combined c_minus_dr and r_plus_d bounds (GT had them separate). Functionally identical.

BOUNDS_PROPAGATION.notes: Entirely mechanical. Two templates: (1) addition→sum_of_limbs_bounded via weaken+sum lemma, (2) multiply/subtract→fe51_limbs_bounded(54) via weaken. Zero cycles across all 7 blocks.

POSTCONDITION_CHAINING.notes: E2 blocks. Required cross-file callee lookup (16 grep/read calls). Model found sibling function (mul_by_pow_2) as template. Block 2 was empty — postconditions chained directly.

SCALAR_BIT_BRIDGE.notes: E3 block. Required finding 5 specific lemmas across 3 files. Most complex single block (38 LOC). Model found all lemmas and arranged them correctly.

ALGEBRAIC_BRIDGE.notes: E1 block included lemma_field51_add call (algebraic bridge) plus bounds. Model found dedicated lemma_add_bounds_propagate instead of using bit_vector assertions.

block_interaction.notes: E4: each block's output bounds were correctly used as input bounds for the next block. No redundant re-assertions. E2: Block 3 correctly relied on Block 1 having established validity.

contamination_analysis.E1: Used extensional equality + lemma_add_bounds_propagate — different from GT (array literal + bit_vector). Found AddAssign in same file as template.
contamination_analysis.E2: Found mul_by_pow_2 as template — different structure from GT. Block 2 empty vs GT 4-line block.
contamination_analysis.E3: Same logical chain as GT but 9 LOC shorter. Better step-by-step organization.
contamination_analysis.E4: Same lemma calls as GT (only 2 lemmas available for this pattern). Block 8 combined vs GT separate — minor structural difference.
contamination_analysis.verdict: No evidence of GT memorization. All solutions show independent reasoning with different structural choices.

performance.full_verification: 1985 verified, 2 errors (both pre-existing in field.rs Sub impl, unrelated)

questions_answered.Q1_block_position_identification: YES — all 4 agents correctly identified block positions from function structure alone. The model understands where proof blocks go between exec statements.
questions_answered.Q2_cross_file_callee_lookup: YES — E2 found ensures in curve_models/mod.rs (16 lookup calls). E3 found lemmas across 3 files (8 calls). Lookup works reliably.
questions_answered.Q3_block_interaction: YES — later blocks correctly use earlier block facts. No redundant assertions. E2 Block 2 was empty because Block 1 established sufficient context.
questions_answered.Q4_bounds_propagation_mechanical: YES — avg 0 cycles across 7 bounds blocks. Entirely template-driven with 2 patterns. Effectively free.
questions_answered.Q5_loop_invariant: NOT TESTED — E3 tested the mul wrapper (SCALAR_BIT_BRIDGE) not the mul_bits_be loop body. The full montgomery ladder loop invariant (~400 proof LOC) is out of scope for this experiment. Design underestimated complexity (~40 LOC estimate vs ~400 actual).

verdict_detail: All 4 functions passed with 0 compile-fix cycles. Exec block generation is reliable for BOUNDS_PROPAGATION, POSTCONDITION_CHAINING, ALGEBRAIC_BRIDGE, and SCALAR_BIT_BRIDGE patterns. LOOP_INVARIANT pattern remains untested. Cross-file callee lookup works. Bounds propagation is fully mechanical. Pipeline's exec block mode (postcondition chaining) is empirically validated.

limitations.loop_invariant_untested: The montgomery ladder loop invariant was not tested. mul_bits_be has ~400 LOC of proof in the loop body, far exceeding the ~40 LOC estimate. This is the highest-risk exec block pattern.
limitations.e3_scoped_to_wrapper: E3 tested the mul wrapper function, not the full mul_bits_be ladder body. The wrapper tests SCALAR_BIT_BRIDGE, not LOOP_INVARIANT.
limitations.small_sample: Only 4 functions, 11 blocks. More data needed for confidence.
limitations.gt_context_available: Surrounding proof blocks (blocks 1-2 and 9+ for E4, sibling functions for E2) provided context. Fully standalone generation was not tested.

recommendations.pipeline_confidence: High for non-loop exec blocks. BOUNDS_PROPAGATION blocks can be generated mechanically with zero cycles.
recommendations.loop_invariant_follow_up: Need separate experiment for loop invariant blocks. Candidate: mul_bits_be loop body (3 blocks, ~230 LOC per iteration). High difficulty.
recommendations.template_recognition: Model effectively uses sibling functions and surrounding blocks as templates. Pipeline should provide these in context.
recommendations.block_ordering: Front-to-back ordering works well. Each block's output becomes implicit context for the next.
